question: What are the admissions deadlines for SUTD?
answer: The application period for local Diploma applicants for SUTD is from January 2 to March 2, 2026. For other admissions, specific deadlines are not provided in the context.

question: Is there financial aid available?
answer: Yes, there is financial aid available at SUTD. Students can apply for scholarships administered by SUTD, including donor-sponsored scholarships, as well as financial aid. For more information, you can contact the Office of Student Administration (OSA) at SUTD.

Tuition Grant eligibility
Students are eligible to take up a Tuition Grant if they have not previously taken a Tuition Grant or received sponsorship/scholarship from the Government of Singapore to attain another qualification at an equal or higher level.
This includes other subsidies by MOE or sponsorship by the Singapore Government (such as government scholarships offered by the Ministries, Public Service Commission and Statutory Boards for local or overseas study).

question: What is the minimum score for the Mother Tongue Language?
answer: The minimum score for the Mother Tongue Language (MTL) requirement is a D7 grade for Higher MTL at the Singapore-Cambridge GCE O-Level.

Admission requirements

International qualifications
Criteria for Admission
You should have completed at least 12 years of general education or have taken the Year 12 national/international examination in the year of application in order to be considered for admission. Students with strong competency in Mathematics and the Sciences, i.e. Physics or Chemistry, are generally preferred.
Mother Tongue Language (MTL)
All Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents are required to fulfil the MTL requirement for admission into full-time publicly-funded undergraduate programmes in the universities.
The MTL requirement may be fulfilled through the following:
a D7 grade for Higher MTL at Singapore-Cambridge GCE O-Level (the iGCSE MTL First Language examination does not fulfil the requirement); or
top_source_2_text:
Applicants who have been away from Singapore’s school system for some years and have not kept up with the study of their MTL or a language that can be offered as MTL-in-lieu may apply for MTL exemption. The MTL exemption application will be facilitated by the universities as part of the university application, and the results made known to applicants who receive admission offers from the university. Please note that MOE does not accept direct applications for MTL exemption. The application for MTL exemption will also not prejudice the evaluation of the application for a place in the university.
English Proficiency requirement
Submission of either IELTS, TOEFL, SAT, Pearson Test of English Academic (PTE Academic), ACT scores or C1 Advanced is compulsory for applicants who are presenting qualifications where English is not the medium of instruction, including (but not limited to):
Indonesian Ijazah SMA
Indonesian Ujian Nasional (UN)/Ebtanas
Myanmar Matriculation Examination

question: Do I require reference letters?
answer: Yes, you are required to provide reference letters as part of your application. You should list up to two referees who know you well academically or professionally, and you need to seek their consent before listing them. Additionally, you must upload any relevant testimonials or recommendation letters.
top_source_1_text:
Recommendation/ Testimonial
List up to two referees and their contact details. Your referee(s) should be persons who know you well academically (e.g. your teacher from High School/Polytechnic) or professionally (your internship or work supervisor) and for at least half a year recently. Do seek your referee(s)’ consent before listing them.
UPLOAD
your school or any other relevant testimonial(s)/recommendation letter(s).
Outstanding/ Relevant achievements
List your past experiences in leadership, involvement in relevant research/ internship/ entrepreneurship programmes, community service, and significant achievements that you consider to be most important. These experiences and achievements should be within the last four years.
UPLOAD
the supporting documents (e.g. SGC) for the achievements listed above.
Share any other interesting information about yourself such as your hobbies and interests. You may submit URLs to your videos on social media websites, such as YouTube.
top_source_2_text:
Contact information
Provide a valid email address (at least 12 months from registration).
Provide a valid contact number.
Academic qualification
Provide details of your academic grades.
UPLOAD
Academic Transcripts and Certificates (at secondary/junior high and senior high school levels).
Supplementary results
This section is optional except for certain International Qualifications.
Key in any additional test results, e.g. English proficiency test, if any or as required.
UPLOAD
a copy of the official test result, if any or as required.
Personal insight questions

a D7 grade for Higher MTL at Singapore-Cambridge GCE O-Level (the iGCSE MTL First Language examination does not fulfil the requirement); or
a pass in MTL ‘B’ or a S grade for H1 MTL/ MTL-in-lieu or H2 MTL Language and Literature or H1 General Studies in Chinese at Singapore-Cambridge GCE A-Level; or
a pass in MTL A: Literature, or MTL A: Language and Literature, or Language B MTL at Standard or Higher Level at International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (the IB Standard Level Language ab initio does not fulfil the requirement).
Those who have not fulfilled the MTL requirement may still apply for admission with no prejudice to their application. However, if accepted, they will be required to fulfil the MTL requirement during their course of study.

question: Can polytechnic diploma students apply?
answer: Yes, polytechnic diploma students can apply to SUTD. Regardless of nationality, applicants with diplomas from specific polytechnics or institutions in Singapore are eligible to apply.

Student profile
As a guide, the University has provided the following reference data to help prospective applicants make an informed choice in applying to the university. Of the Polytechnic students who were offered in the university admission exercise in 2025:
Most of our offers are for those whose diplomas are closely aligned with SUTD’s programmes, such as Engineering, Computing, Information Technology, Artificial Intelligence, Architecture and Sciences.
Most have strong results in STEM subjects, as well as project/studio components.
And had also taken the Singapore-Cambridge GCE O-Level, most had scored As or Bs in Mathematics (Additional Mathematics and/or Mathematics) and Sciences (Physics and/or Chemistry).

question: Do I need SAT score?
answer: SAT scores are optional for admission to SUTD. However, if you choose to submit them, you should indicate the SUTD Institution Code (6532) on your SAT registration forms so that the scores will be sent directly to the university.

question: How many PhD students does SUTD have?
answer: The information regarding the number of PhD students at SUTD is not available in the provided documents.
top_source_1_text: SUTD offers a truly unconventional education with an interdisciplinary pedagogy. All students, regardless of majors, are exposed to integrating knowledge from all disciplines throughout all levels of study as part of their training. Our curriculum provides students with a strong foundation in science, mathematics and technology to build foundational literacy for future careers and endeavours. From 2026 onwards, with AI woven into all three terms of the first-year (Freshmore) curriculum, all SUTD students will receive comprehensive training in Design and AI skills to prepare them for the new world.
top_source_2_text:
Are all diplomas accepted for admission?
Generally, diplomas from the School of Engineering, Information Technology, Architecture or Sciences are considered more relevant to SUTD’s courses and hence will be assessed more favourably for admission. However, other diplomas may still be considered on a case-by-case basis.

question: How much are the tuition fees for Singaporeans?
answer:
The tuition fees for Singapore Citizens (SC) at SUTD are as follows:

- **Academic Year 2023**: 
  - Subsidised Fee: S$13,500 per academic year (S$6,750 per term)
  
- **Academic Year 2022**: 
  - Subsidised Fee: S$13,500 per academic year (S$6,750 per term)
  
- **Academic Year 2021**: 
  - Subsidised Fee: S$13,300 per academic year (S$6,650 per term)
  
- **Academic Year 2020**: 
  - Subsidised Fee: S$13,200 per academic year (S$6,600 per term)

- **Academic Year 2026** (for new students): 
  - Subsidised Fee: S$13,200 per academic year (S$6,600 per term)

These fees are inclusive of GST and are applicable for students who take up the Tuition Grant scheme.

question: How much are the tuition fees for international students?
answer:
The tuition fees for international students at SUTD are as follows:

- **Per academic year (Non-Subsidised Fee)**: S$62,076
- **Per term (Non-Subsidised Fee)**: S$31,038

For subsidised fees, international students (ASEAN) pay S$30,200 per academic year and S$15,100 per term. 

Please note that these fees are inclusive of GST.

question: Is there a minimum CAP?
answer: Yes, there is a minimum Cumulative Average Point (CAP) requirement. From AY2017 onwards, students must maintain a CAP of 3.5 or above to be eligible to enroll for an additional course in Term 5, 6, or 7.
top_source_1_text:
Students are required to take a HASS elective every term from Term 4 onwards.
Capstone (24 Credits)
The capstone is a culminating project that allows students to use the skills they have learned in ISTD in a real world industry or research project. The capstone projects often focus on interdisciplinary applications, solved by a team of students chosen appropriately from different pillars.
Extra Courses
From AY2017 onwards, students who have, and maintain a CGPA of 3.5 or above (and passed all Freshmore subjects) are eligible to enrol for an additional course in Term 5, 6 or 7. Subject to the prerequisites and timing of the course, students may use this flexibility to take an additional HASS or elective course.
The following diagram depicts the typical sequence of courses in the CSD curriculum.

question: What do I actually need to submit for my portfolio?
answer:
To submit your portfolio for SUTD, you need to include the following:

1. **Contact Information**: A valid email address and contact number.
2. **Academic Qualifications**: 
   - Details of your academic grades.
   - Upload academic transcripts and certificates from secondary/junior high and senior high school levels.
3. **Supplementary Results** (optional, except for certain International Qualifications): 
   - Additional test results, such as English proficiency tests, if required.
   - Upload a copy of the official test result, if applicable.
4. **Personal Insight Questions**: Responses that provide new and additional information about yourself.
5. **Recommendation/Testimonial**: 
   - List up to two referees with their contact details.
   - Upload any relevant testimonials or recommendation letters.
6. **Outstanding/Relevant Achievements**: 
   - List experiences in leadership, research, internships, community service, and significant achievements from the last four years.
   - Upload supporting documents for these achievements.
7. **Other Information**: 
   - Indicate your interest in one of the majors.
   - Share details of any enrolment in another university, past convictions, or any special assistance required.
8. **Scholarships and Financial Aid**: 
   - Declare your gross monthly household income and indicate if you wish to apply for scholarships or financial aid.
9. **Special Programmes** (optional): 
   - Indicate interest in any special programmes and rank

question: How are roommates matched in the hostel?
answer: Roommates in the hostel are matched based on the class or cluster they are assigned to. Additionally, students complete a lifestyle questionnaire as part of the housing acceptance process, which helps to pair them with roommates who have similar lifestyle habits as far as possible.
top_source_1_text:
Room type: Double Room
Freshmore students are paired in double rooms according to the class/cluster they are assigned to. Students complete a lifestyle questionnaire as part of the housing acceptance process, and will be matched with roommates of similar lifestyle habits as far as possible.
Hostel facilities
Communal shower and bathroom facilities on each floor
Mini pantry with microwave, hot/cold water dispenser and sink on each floor
Communal kitchen (level 1) equipped with induction cookers, multifunction oven and vending machines
Pantry (level 9) with a 2‐burner induction cooker, microwave, hot water dispenser and vending machines
Laundry room (level 1) with washing machines and dryers (pay-per-use basis)
Laundry drying area (level 12)
Hostel lounge (level 1, block 57)
Viewing deck (level 12, blocks 55 and 59)
Recreational rooms
Study and discussion rooms
LAN point and WIFI
top_source_2_text:
Residents’ safety and welfare
SUTD reviews its security measures periodically to ensure the safety and well-being of our hostel residents, and has strengthened its security measures at the hostel over the years. This includes a 24/7 on-site security team, regular security patrols, monitoring of CCTVs (closed circuit television) and controlled access to blocks, common facilities and bathrooms. Residents are also regularly reminded on issues regarding their personal safety and security.
In addition, live-in staff and hostel student leaders play an important role in the education, outreach and engagement of student residents. Together, they help to foster a culture of care and respect within the hostel community. By bringing residents’ concerns to various teams at the University, their presence helps the University to have a better appreciation of the issues of importance to residents. Resident students are also able to reach out to them easily if they are in need of assistance.
top_source_3_text:
From Academic Year 2026, Freshmore undergraduates are required to reside at student housing on campus during Terms 1 to 3. Designed as an integrated part of the Freshmore experience, residential living complements cohort-based learning in and out of classrooms and aims to foster a sense of community and ownership, building strong friendships that carry through the undergraduate years and beyond.
Freshmore students who would like to stay on after Term 3 may apply for housing together with other undergraduates during scheduled housing application windows, typically in February each year. Information on the housing application window will be published on the
Undergraduate housing page
by early February. Housing allocation will be guided by the Undergraduate Housing Allocation Scheme. More information on the scheme will be shared with incoming Freshmores after the start of the September term.

question: What does the school like out for in applicants?
answer:
SUTD looks for a comprehensive application review that includes the following aspects in applicants:

1. **Academic Competency**: A strong focus on Mathematics and the Sciences, particularly in the context of the opportunities afforded to applicants. This includes final year exam results and academic performance over the 2-3 years prior to the final exam.

2. **Qualities and Traits**: Applicants should demonstrate qualities that align with SUTD's values, such as:
   - **Growth Mindset**: Embracing challenges, learning from feedback, and demonstrating continuous improvement.
   - **Collaboration**: Working effectively with others, valuing diverse perspectives, and contributing actively to team success.
   - **Curiosity/Open-mindedness**: A willingness to explore and learn.

3. **Portfolios**: This may include co-curricular activities and other relevant experiences that showcase the applicant's skills and interests.

Overall, SUTD assesses students holistically, considering both academic performance and personal qualities.

question: If I don't have a good MTL grade, can I still get in?
answer: Yes, you can still apply for admission even if you don't have a good Mother Tongue Language (MTL) grade. However, if accepted, you will be required to fulfill the MTL requirement during your course of study.

question: What exactly is the Computer Science course in SUTD about?
answer:
The Computer Science course at SUTD, known as Computer Science and Design (ISTD), is part of a modern, interdisciplinary approach to education. It focuses on integrating knowledge from various disciplines, particularly emphasizing the relationship between technology and design. The curriculum prepares students with a strong foundation in science, mathematics, and technology, while also incorporating training in Design and AI skills starting from 2026.

Students in this program will learn to leverage AI thoughtfully to create effective, human-centered solutions. The course includes project-based learning, where students will generate high-fidelity Computer-Aided Design (CAD) files, produce functional code, and conduct strategic analyses. This approach ensures that graduates are equipped not only with technical skills but also with critical thinking and an understanding of societal needs, making them well-prepared for future careers in a rapidly evolving technological landscape.
top_source_1_text: SUTD offers a truly unconventional education with an interdisciplinary pedagogy. All students, regardless of majors, are exposed to integrating knowledge from all disciplines throughout all levels of study as part of their training. Our curriculum provides students with a strong foundation in science, mathematics and technology to build foundational literacy for future careers and endeavours. From 2026 onwards, with AI woven into all three terms of the first-year (Freshmore) curriculum, all SUTD students will receive comprehensive training in Design and AI skills to prepare them for the new world.
top_source_2_text:
Equip yourself for the future with a foundation in digital competency, through our curriculum enriched with data science and powered by Design·AI.
Compulsory classes in Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (HASS) also elevate your inquiry, analysis, interpretation and presentation skills, shaping you into an
innovation graduate
with critical thinking and a good understanding of societal needs.

question: What if my English is not good?
answer: If your English is not good, you will need to submit proof of English proficiency as part of your application. This can be done by providing scores from tests such as IELTS, TOEFL, SAT, Pearson Test of English Academic (PTE Academic), ACT, or C1 Advanced. This requirement applies to applicants whose qualifications were not taught in English. If you have not fulfilled the English proficiency requirement, you may still apply for admission, but you will need to meet this requirement if accepted.
